title: rubus × tomentellifolius
intro: Rubus × tomentellifolius, also known as the hybrid black raspberry, is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its high content of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its dark purple color. The health benefits of Rubus × tomentellifolius include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection, as well as its ability to support digestive health and immune function. Rubus × tomentellifolius is often prepared as a jam, jelly, or syrup, and is also used in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
